signals.title: USD/JPY — Geopolitical Headline + Carry
signals.text: USD/JPY at 157.05 — 300bp Fed-BoJ carry sustains the bid as Middle East tensions escalate following reports of imminent US/Israeli strikes on Iran. JPY COT LF net short -88,543 (30.6% long) remains crowded; while VIX at 18.3 indicates normal volatility, the threat to the Strait of Hormuz introduces a risk-off USD bid that competes with JPY safe-haven flows. Carry dominates the regime until VIX exceeds 25, but intervention risk remains the primary ceiling near 160.00.

signals.title: NZD/USD — COT vs OIS Divergence + Geopolitical Shock
signals.text: NZD/USD at 0.5878 — COT LF net short -16,833 (23.0% long) shows speculators fading the RBNZ hawkish bias (Hike bias active). Reports of missile strikes in the UAE have triggered a flight to USD, pressuring the pair despite the 150bp USD carry advantage. If the RBNZ maintains its hike stance on 27 May, the crowded spec short position faces significant unwind risk; however, immediate downside is driven by geopolitical risk-off flows.

signals.title: AUD/USD — Positioning + Retail Divergence
signals.text: AUD/USD at 0.7176 — COT LF net long +48,299 (74.8% long) vs retail 83% short. Institutional length is testing extreme levels while retail traders aggressively fade the trend. RBA at 4.10% vs Fed 3.75% provides a 35bp AUD carry premium, but Middle East noise and a 2.41% drop in Gold prices limit upside ahead of the RBA meeting on 5 May. Retail capitulation risk rises if price holds above 0.7200.

signals.title: EUR/USD — Correlation Break + Fed Commentary
signals.text: EUR/USD at 1.1703 — Correlation vs EuroStoxx at +0.724 (z=+1.89σ) indicates a significant break from the -0.142 norm; EUR is tracking equity sentiment rather than traditional macro drivers. Fed's Williams boosting inflation views supports the 175bp USD carry advantage (Fed 3.75% vs ECB 2.0%). EUR COT LF net short -18,293 suggests neutral-to-bearish institutional sentiment. Watch for a return to the historical inverse correlation if USD funding stress intensifies.
